summary: Unmerged tooling PR that proposed replacing the root build shell workflow with an installable Python CLI command named `melee`. It added a `tools/cli` package with an argparse-based `build`/`b` subcommand mirroring build.sh-style options for clean, rebuild, expected sync, map generation, NON_MATCHING/SKIP_CHECK, EPILOGUE_PROCESS, logging, DOL dumping, jobs, and formatting. The PR was closed with the author noting it would be revisited after moving to `ninja`.
what_changed: Added an editable Python package under `tools/cli` and wired it into `requirements.txt` with `-e tools/cli`.
Added a console entry point `melee = main:main`, intended to expose repository command-line tooling through a single CLI.
Added `melee build` with alias `melee b`, using argparse subparsers.

Implemented build options corresponding to existing workflow flags: `--clean`, `--rebuild`, `--expected`, `--frank`, `--map`, `--non-matching`, `--skip-check`, `--clear`, `--log`, `--dump`, `--jobs`, and `--pretty`.

The build command shells out to `make`, optionally runs `make format` and `make clean`, passes make variables such as `NON_MATCHING=1`, `SKIP_CHECK=1`, `EPILOGUE_PROCESS=1`, and `GENERATE_MAP=1`, and can pass `-j`.
Added post-build map handling: if `build/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map` exists, the command imports `tools/parse_map.py` and runs its `main` to produce `build/map.csv`.
Added optional DOL dump integration using `dadosod dol ... -m build/map.csv -o dump`.
Added optional syncing from `build` to `expected`, and from `dump` to `expected`, using `rsync -a --delete` after successful builds.
Added simple path helpers in `tools/cli/utils.py` for repo root, build path, and expected path.
Added Black configuration through `pyproject.toml` with line length 80 and added `line_length = 80` to `.editorconfig`.
Reformatted `tools/parse_map.py` with Black; the review comment says there were no intentional logic changes to that file.
Added AGPLv3 license text and setuptools metadata for the new CLI package.
smart_moves: Used a single `melee` command as a planned umbrella entry point for loose decomp scripts, reducing the need for contributors to remember many script paths.
Kept `build` and short alias `b`, matching the PR body's shown help output and making common build usage concise.
Preserved existing build workflow knobs rather than forcing contributors to learn a new model all at once.
Encapsulated repo-relative paths in helper functions, which is safer than scattering path assumptions through build command code.
Integrated map parsing and optional DOL dumping as post-build steps, reflecting common decomp workflows around maps and asm dumps.
Made formatting configuration explicit with Black line length 80, reducing future Python formatting churn.
Documented the intended future CLI expansion in the PR body, including possible `melee diff` and `melee dump` entry points.
decomp_lessons: For decomp repositories, build tooling should preserve existing environment toggles such as `NON_MATCHING`, `SKIP_CHECK`, map generation, and expected-output syncing when replacing scripts.
A CLI wrapper can make high-friction decomp tasks discoverable through `-h` help output, especially for options like map generation, object rebuilds, and DOL dumping.
When refactoring build scripts, keep map parsing and dump generation close to the build command because they are part of the verify-and-inspect loop for matching work.
Formatting-only changes should be explicitly labeled; here `tools/parse_map.py` was called out as Black-only to reduce review ambiguity.
Large build-tool rewrites may need to wait for underlying build-system changes; this PR was closed with a note to revisit after moving to `ninja`.
Generated packaging metadata such as `egg-info` appeared in the diff; future tooling PRs should decide deliberately whether such generated files belong in-tree.
naming_conventions: Python package/distribution naming appeared as `melee_cli` / `melee-cli` in setup metadata.
Build flags used long descriptive argparse names with hyphenated CLI spelling, such as `--non-matching` and `--skip-check`.
Make variables remained uppercase with existing project names: `NON_MATCHING`, `SKIP_CHECK`, `EPILOGUE_PROCESS`, and `GENERATE_MAP`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Supports deleting selected object files under `build` via `--rebuild` glob pattern, allowing targeted recompilation without a full clean.
Supports `--map` to request map generation with `GENERATE_MAP=1`.
Automatically parses `GALE01.map` into `build/map.csv` when the map exists.
Supports `--dump` to dump `main.dol` to asm with `dadosod` using the generated map CSV.
Supports syncing successful build artifacts into `expected`, useful for expected/build workflows.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: No changes to this file, just ran `black`.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: PR was closed with the note: `Will revisit this after moving to ninja`.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that moved the project build from make/build.sh toward ninja.
Find later accepted CLI or tooling PRs that reused or replaced the `melee` command idea.
Check whether generated `tools/cli/melee_cli.egg-info` files were removed or avoided in later packaging work.
Compare current build tooling flags against the options proposed in `tools/cli/commands/build.py`.
Find later changes to `tools/parse_map.py` after this Black-only reformat.
